ksa_text: Ability to develop, document, communicate, and iteratively update governance policies that reflect organizational objectives, regulatory requirements, and evolving AI capabilities.

dcwf.top_element_label: Interpret and apply applicable laws, statutes, and regulatory documents and integrate into policy.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Interpret and apply applicable laws, statutes, and regulatory documents and integrate into policy.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI Governance is a primary or secondary domain for the GenAI CoE Leader. This ability KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (c suite).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (AI Governance), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.507). External reviewers may question whether this specific ability KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the GenAI CoE Leader versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: NIST AI RMF, EU AI Act (Article 9), and OECD AI Principles confirm AI governance as a critical competency for responsible AI leadership.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with AI Governance appearing in job postings and role definition literature for GenAI CoE Leader.

decision_rationale: AI Governance ability KSA is within scope for the GenAI CoE Leader at the c suite tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
